research.status_summary: Substack is actively hiring (~17 open roles as of mid-2026), independently operating as a $1.1B unicorn with no acquisition or layoff signals.
research.hiring_policy: Roles are predominantly US-based (San Francisco HQ) with no public evidence of visa sponsorship or EOR-friendly international hiring; Substack does not appear on curated visa-sponsorship/relocation job lists, making it a poor fit for internationally-distributed candidates.
recent_news: March 2026: Launched built-in desktop Recording Studio for creators to pre-record/publish video with auto-generated clips and thumbnails; creators using video features grew revenue 50% faster than non-video peers.
January 2026: Launched Substack TV, a standalone app for Apple TV and Google TV supporting video posts and livestreams with a TikTok-style 'For You' recommendations feed.
February 2026: Disclosed an October 2025 security breach in which a third party accessed user email addresses, phone numbers, and internal metadata — now patched.
Platform reached 8.4M paid subscribers and 17,000+ revenue-earning writers as of mid-2026, up from 5M paid subscriptions reported in March 2025 (67% YoY growth).
Substack remains deliberately AI-cautious on content generation, positioning itself as the 'home of authentic human writing,' while using AI only for discovery/analytics features.
research.growth_signal: Post-$100M Series C (July 2025, a16z + BOND, $1.1B valuation), Substack is aggressively shipping multimedia product (Recording Studio, Substack TV) and expanding its paid creator base — not yet profitable but on a strong revenue trajectory.
research.fit_for_lior: Substack is a strong contextual match for Lior's Creative AI and creator-economy threads as a research/writing beat, but its US-centric, small-team (~100 employees) hiring posture with no EOR or visa sponsorship makes it a low-probability direct employer for a globally-distributed candidate.
